behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is template-lint logic (string/pattern/AST comparison), not runtime wiring. MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about one checker’s decision, so Unit is sufficient. Rejected Integration because behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check" and does not require harness assembly or model invocation. Adversarial critique: if lint must read many real skill files with path-resolution edge cases, this could be Integration instead.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a template fixture that includes phase instructions copied from SKILL.md content and assert it returns a rejection with precise location/reason.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Level matches test: pure in-process checker test, no I/O required.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolation + fixtures + assembler). Unit mocks can miss leakage introduced during assembly. Rejected System because behavior text is about "fully assembled prompt" content, not OS/network/subprocess effects. Adversarial critique: if assembly depends on shell/env/subprocess templating, System may be required.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt-builder pipeline with representative fixtures, then assert final assembled prompt excludes forbidden phase-instruction fragments derived from SKILL.md.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses real harness wiring (multiple modules), which justifies Integration.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is wiring/source-of-truth drift across loader + invoker + validator modules. No required real OS/network boundary in behavior text; local filesystem/module integration is enough. Rejected Agentic because this behavior can be proven by source provenance and controlled context plumbing, while output-quality dependence is covered explicitly by behaviors 5 and 8. Adversarial critique: if "validates behavior" here means model judgment correctness, minimum should be Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation path with a skill-file fixture containing unique sentinel guidance; assert loaded context is injected from files and validator checks are tied to that context, with no hard-coded duplicate rules in prompt template.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is necessary to cover loader/invoker/validator interaction.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness configurability and fixture switching across modules/files; deterministic and infrastructure-local. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about ability to run unchanged prompt across skill variants, not model output quality. Adversarial critique: if variant switching uses real process isolation/container mounts, System might be required.
behaviors.test_description: Run two harness invocations (baseline skill, degraded/missing skill) with identical user prompt string; assert prompt text is unchanged and only skill context source differs by selected variant.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises real harness wiring and file-variant selection, matching Integration.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing when skill context regresses; stubs can mask this by returning fixed outputs. LLM-DEP applies directly. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single model-response comparison under one prompt, not multiple agentic stages. Adversarial critique: if failure is triggered purely by static preflight checks before model call, Integration could suffice.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model with identical user prompt against baseline and degraded SKILL.md variants; assert degraded variant violates structural behavior expectation that baseline satisfies.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real LLM call to expose regression sensitivity to skill-context quality.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering/instrumentation across model client wrapper + artifact writer + parser modules. Can be caught with in-process wiring and local filesystem artifact checks. Rejected System because no subprocess/network behavior is required beyond normal local execution. Adversarial critique: if artifact durability depends on real async process crashes/signals, System may be needed.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness with a controlled model response and force parser failure; assert raw response artifact exists and timestamp/order indicates write happened before parse/extract step.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test reflects cross-module sequencing and artifact emission.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is assertion logic itself (validator semantics), which is deterministic and local. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on constraint type, not harness/module interactions. Adversarial critique: if assertions are declarative configs merged at runtime from multiple files, Integration may be safer.
behaviors.test_description: Feed validator crafted outputs that pass keyword checks but violate schema-path/role-placement rules; assert structural validator fails while keyword-only baseline would pass.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Pure validator behavior can be verified without external dependencies.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and depends on real model output characteristics where keyword signals can be misleading. Mocked outputs would not prove real regression-catch capability. Rejected Workflow because this is one model output evaluation path, not multi-step agent pipeline. Adversarial critique: if regressions are replayed from fixed historical transcripts, Integration could catch them without live calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test on prompt/skill setup known to produce subtle mis-structured outputs; verify structural assertions fail while keyword-only check still passes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description needs real LLM output variance/quality, matching Agentic.
